items.q: Why is HDI a better measure of development than GNI per capita?
items.correct: HDI combines income, life expectancy and education, capturing quality of life — GNI per capita is just average income and hides inequality and non-economic wellbeing.
items.misconception: Because HDI measures how happy people are.
items.random_wrong: Because HDI is easier to calculate than GNI.
items.why: Students treat HDI as a happiness index. It is broader than GNI but still only captures three measurable dimensions — not subjective wellbeing or environmental quality.

items.q: Why do earthquakes typically cause more deaths in LICs than HICs?
items.correct: LICs often have poorly enforced building codes, weak emergency response, dense informal housing and limited preparedness — not because the earthquakes are stronger.
items.misconception: LICs have more earthquake deaths because more earthquakes happen there.
items.random_wrong: HICs never have earthquake deaths because of advanced technology.
items.why: Vulnerability is socio-economic, not seismic. Japan and Haiti both sit on plate boundaries; the death toll difference comes from building quality, planning and response — a key development comparison.

items.correct: Development that meets the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs — balancing economic, social and environmental goals.
items.misconception: Sustainable development means stopping all economic growth.
items.random_wrong: Sustainable development means using only solar power.
items.why: Students treat sustainability as anti-development. The Brundtland definition is about balance and intergenerational equity — not zero growth or one specific technology.

items.q: Which is a Newly Emerging Economy (NEE)?
items.why: It's Nigeria. “Norway” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: HDI combines income, schooling and which third measure?
items.why: Correct answer: Life expectancy. “Population” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: What is the maximum value of HDI?
items.why: The real figure is 1. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
